assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: task-1 (trustify-backend), task-2 (trustify-backend), task-3 (trustify-backend), task-4 (trustify-backend), task-9 (trustify-backend), task-10 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: task-5 (trustify-ui), task-6 (trustify-ui), task-7 (trustify-ui), task-8 (trustify-ui). Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend implementation tasks are numbered 2, 3, 4, 9. Frontend tasks are numbered 5, 6, 7, 8. Task 5 (frontend API layer) depends on Task 4 (backend endpoint), which has a lower number. Task 9 (backend integration tests) does not have frontend tasks depending on it. The core dependency chain is: backend tasks 2->3->4 (numbers 2,3,4) come before frontend tasks 5->6->7 (numbers 5,6,7) that depend on them.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 6 (SbomComparePage) references Figma design context ('following the Figma design', 'Badge colors match Figma') and mentions specific PatternFly components (Select, ExpandableSection, Badge, Table, EmptyState, Skeleton, Dropdown, CodeBranchIcon). However, Task 7 (SbomListPage selection) is a UI-facing task (adds checkboxes and a button to a page) and does NOT reference Figma design context at all — it mentions PatternFly components but has zero 'Figma' or 'design' references. Task 5 (API types/hooks) and Task 8 (test fixtures) are API-layer tasks and are exempt.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 5 (trustify-ui) explicitly depends on Task 4 (trustify-backend): 'Depends on: Task 4 — Add GET /api/v2/sbom/compare endpoint (API contract dependency)'. Additionally, all intermediate tasks target the shared feature branch TC-9003, and backend tasks (2,3,4) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (5,6,7,8).

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks reference paths consistent with repo-backend.md structure: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/, common/src/error.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, entity/src/, tests/api/. Frontend tasks reference paths consistent with repo-frontend.md structure: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/, src/pages/SbomListPage/, src/pages/SbomComparePage/ (new, follows the page directory convention), src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/routes.tsx, tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/mocks/fixtures/. New files follow the established directory patterns from the manifests.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 tasks have a '## Repository' section with exactly one repository name. Tasks 1,2,3,4,9,10 list 'trustify-backend'; tasks 5,6,7,8 list 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 tasks (task-1 through task-10) contain all four required sections: '## Target Branch', '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria', '## Test Requirements'. Verified by checking each file for these section headers.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Backend Files to Modify reference existing paths from repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs. Backend Files to Create follow the same directory patterns: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs (follows model/ pattern), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/compare.rs (follows service/ pattern), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/compare.rs (follows endpoints/ pattern), tests/api/sbom_compare.rs (follows tests/api/ pattern). Frontend Files to Modify reference existing paths: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/routes.tsx, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, tests/mocks/handlers.ts. Frontend Files to Create follow established patterns: src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts (follows hooks/ pattern), src/pages/SbomComparePage/ (follows pages/ directory convention), tests/mocks/fixtures/sbom-comparison.json (follows fixtures/ pattern).

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Bookend tasks (task-1, task-10) correctly omit Implementation Notes. All non-bookend tasks (2-9) include Implementation Notes with specific file paths and code patterns: Task 2 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs' and 'details.rs' for derivation patterns. Task 3 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs' for service patterns and lists specific entity files. Task 4 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs' and 'list.rs' for handler patterns. Task 5 references 'src/api/client.ts', 'src/hooks/useSboms.ts' for hook patterns. Task 6 references 'src/hooks/useSboms.ts', 'src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx', specific PatternFly components. Task 7 references 'SbomListPage.tsx' and PatternFly Table patterns. Task 8 references 'tests/mocks/handlers.ts' and fixture files. Task 9 references 'tests/api/sbom.rs' for test patterns.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks (task-1 create-branch, task-10 merge-branch) have Target Branch set to 'main'. All intermediate tasks (2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9) have Target Branch set to 'TC-9003' (the feature issue ID). This matches the feature-branch workflow pattern.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains any reference to 'sha256', 'digest', or '[sdlc-workflow]'. Grep across all output files returned zero matches. There are no digest comments in any of the output files.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task output contains any convention references in the prescribed format. Grep for 'convention' and 'CONVENTIONS' in all output files found only two incidental uses: task-8 mentions 'naming conventions' (referring to fixture naming patterns, not CONVENTIONS.md enrichment) and task-5 mentions 'field conventions' (referring to interface naming patterns). No task contains 'Per CONVENTIONS.md', 'Applies: task modifies', or any other evidence of convention-aware enrichment with applicability rationales.
